memory_description: Born in hometown - same village as BaBa
notes: Starting point of life near BaBa

memory_description: Living next door to BaBa's house daily
notes: Grew up in close proximity

memory_description: Regular visits to BaBa's house as toddler
notes: BaBa always awake before me

memory_description: BaBa prepares Mont T breakfast with tea or coffee every morning
notes: Favorite breakfast memory

memory_description: Running to BaBa after being disciplined by father
notes: BaBa's house as safe refuge

memory_description: Listening to BaBa's radio - rare luxury in village
notes: Radio was the only entertainment in town

memory_description: BaBa and Grandma Cho share life stories at bedtime
notes: Life wisdom passed through stories

memory_description: BaBa shares cookies and snacks received from villagers
notes: Generous sharing habit extended to brothers too

memory_description: BaBa applies medicine when injured after punishment
notes: Physical and emotional healing provided

memory_description: Sleeping over at BaBa's house feeling safe and loved
notes: Strongest sense of belonging in childhood

memory_description: Morning wake-up ritual with radio music and breakfast
notes: Best mornings of childhood

memory_description: BaBa gives personal clothing whenever expressed a need
notes: Generosity with his most prized possessions

memory_description: BaBa talks about clothes and furniture and not living long when drunk
notes: Bittersweet - drinking habit noticed with concern

memory_description: BaBa gives his pre-marriage shirt for school festival
notes: Old-fashioned shirt caused laughter but was given with pure love

memory_description: Tried many times to stop BaBa from drinking traditional alcohol
notes: Genuine worry about BaBa's long-term health

memory_description: Moved to Monywa for high school - first major separation from BaBa
major_life_event: Moved to Monywa for high school
notes: Visits reduced to school holiday periods only

memory_description: Visits during Myanmar festivals - Thingyan and Thadingyut
notes: Festival visits felt more precious due to rarity

memory_description: Passed matriculation exam - returned home to share news with BaBa
notes: BaBa proud of academic milestone

memory_description: Started university in Mandalay - even further from hometown
notes: Down to only 4 to 5 visits per year

memory_description: Brief festival visits - BaBa repeatedly asks about graduation timeline
notes: BaBa sighed hearing 5 more years to graduate

memory_description: Short visits during Thadingyut and Thingyan festivals only
notes: Distance and busyness slowly widening the gap

memory_description: Planned to buy BaBa a good liquor bottle after midterm holidays
notes: Promise was never fulfilled - source of deep lasting regret

memory_description: BaBa suffered sudden stroke and passed away at age 72 in November 2020
notes: Most painful and defining moment of this entire life journey

memory_description: Quit university due to Myanmar military coup and joined Civil Disobedience Movement
major_life_event: Myanmar coup - left university - joined CDM
notes: BaBa's absence deeply felt during national crisis and personal uncertainty

memory_description: Living through coup aftermath - reflecting on BaBa's lessons about impermanence
notes: BaBa's words about Samsara and impermanence resonated with lived reality

memory_description: Enrolled at Parami University in Yangon - rebuilding academic journey
notes: New chapter while carrying BaBa's memory and values forward

memory_description: Relocated to Chiang Mai Thailand for security reasons - continued studies abroad
notes: Resilience inherited from BaBa - adapting to new environments

memory_description: Continuing studies in Chiang Mai - BaBa's memory and lessons remain guiding force
notes: Journey continues - Samsara theme of impermanence woven through every life stage
